Yolulo Operations
Promotions

Promotions & Coupons

How to create and manage discount coupons — Product Discounts, Order Discounts, Delivery Fee Discounts, Buy X Get Y, and Free Gifts — including eligibility, usage limits, active dates, and coupon stacking rules

Platform Hyperzod Admin
Section Promotions
Version 1.0
Updated March 2026
← Hub Coupons Product Discount Order Discount Delivery Fee Buy X Get Y Common Settings
Promotions Overview

The Promotions Area

Navigate to Promotions in the left sidebar. The area has four tabs: Coupons, Popups, Push Notifications, and Highlight. This SOP covers Coupons.

Fig 1.1 — Coupons list: tabs for Coupons / Popups / Push Notifications / Highlight. Table columns: Coupon Code, Discount Type, Discount Value, Usage Limit, Usage Count, Status, Start Date, End Date, Actions

Fig 1.2 — Select Discount Type modal: 5 options — Product Discount, Order Discount, Delivery Fee Discount, Buy X Get Y, Free Gift

Coupons List Columns
Coupon Code
The code customers enter at checkout
Discount Type
Which type of discount — Product, Order, Delivery Fee, Buy X Get Y, Free Gift
Discount Value
The discount amount or percentage
Usage Limit
Maximum number of times the coupon can be used — blank = no limit
Usage Count
How many times it has been used so far
Status
Active or Inactive
Start / End Date
When the coupon is valid — no end date = runs indefinitely
Actions
Edit or delete the coupon
The 5 Discount Types
Product Discount
Percentage or fixed amount off specific products or categories
Order Discount
Reduces the total amount of the entire order by a fixed amount or percentage
Delivery Fee Discount
Offers free or discounted delivery fee on customer orders
Buy X Get Y
Give a free or discounted product when customers buy specific products or quantities
Free Gift
Unlock a free gift when the cart meets the minimum spend
Creating a New Coupon
Step 1
Click + Create New (top right of the Coupons page)
Step 2
Select the discount type from the modal — choose based on what you want to offer
Step 3
Complete all required fields in the creation form — a live Discount Summary panel on the right shows a preview as you build
Step 4
Click + Save (top right) — the coupon appears in the Coupons list immediately
Shared Settings

Settings Common to All Discount Types

Every discount type shares the same top section (Discount Type, code, settings) and bottom sections (eligible customers, order types, usage limits, combine rules, active dates). These are documented once here.

Fig CS.1 — Discount Type section: Coupon Code (selected) vs Automatic Discount radio, Coupon Code field (required), Description, Public Coupon and Active Status checkboxes

Fig CS.2 — Coupon Code field with autocomplete showing "Yolulo" suggestion

Discount Type
Coupon Code
Customer must enter this code at checkout to redeem the discount. The code field becomes required
Automatic Discount
Applied automatically to eligible orders — no code needed. Customer never sees a code
Discount Settings
Coupon Code Required
Enter a memorable, uppercase code — e.g. YOLULO10, FREESHIP. Must be unique. Autocomplete may suggest existing codes
Description
Internal note — not shown to customers. Use to describe the promotion purpose
Public Coupon
When enabled: coupon is publicly discoverable by customers. When disabled: only redeemable by customers who know the exact code
Active Status
When enabled: coupon is live and usable. When disabled: coupon is saved but inactive — useful for scheduling or pausing
Eligible Customers (all types)
All customers
Default — any customer can use this coupon
Specific segments
Restrict to a named customer segment (e.g. VIP Customers) — segments must be created first in the Customers area
Specific customers
Restrict to individually selected customer accounts
Eligible Order Types & Payment Methods (all types)
All Order Types toggle
ON = applies to both Delivery and Pickup. Toggle OFF to restrict to specific order types
All Payment Methods toggle
ON = applies to all payment methods. Toggle OFF to restrict to specific payment modes (e.g. Stripe only)
Set Discount Usage Limits (all types)
Limit total uses
Tick to set a maximum number of times this coupon can be redeemed across all customers — enter the number when checked
Limit uses per customer
Tick to restrict how many times a single customer can use it — enter the number when checked
No limits
Leave both unchecked for unlimited usage
Combine with Other Discounts (all types)
Product discounts
Allow this coupon to stack with active product discount coupons
Order discounts
Allow this coupon to stack with active order discount coupons
Delivery fee discounts
Allow this coupon to stack with active delivery fee discount coupons (shown on Order and Product discount forms)
Active Dates (all types)
Start Date Required
Date the coupon becomes active — defaults to today
Start Time
Time on the start date the coupon activates — defaults to current time
Set end date
Tick to add an expiry date and time — leave unchecked for no end date (runs indefinitely)
💡
The Discount Summary panel on the right updates live as you fill in the form — use it to verify the coupon is configured correctly before saving.
Discount Type 1

Product Discount

Apply a percentage or fixed amount off specific products or categories at a specific merchant. Ideal for promoting individual items or clearing stock.

Fig PD.1 — Product Discount: Discount Value section — Type dropdown (Percentage selected), Percentage % field, Max Discount Amount (optional)

Fig PD.2 — Applies To section: Merchant search dropdown, Apply To field (greyed until merchant selected). Minimum Purchase Requirements: No minimum / Minimum purchase amount / Minimum quantity

Fig PD.3 — Eligible Customers: All customers / Specific segments / Specific customers. Eligible Order Types & Payment Methods with All Order Types and All Payment Methods toggles

Fig PD.4 — Combine with other discounts: Product discounts / Order discounts / Delivery fee discounts checkboxes. Active Dates: Start Date + Start Time + Set end date

Product Discount — Unique Fields
Discount Value → Type
Percentage (%) or Fixed Amount — select from dropdown
Percentage (%)
Enter the discount percentage — e.g. 10 for 10% off
Max Discount Amount
Optional cap — e.g. if 20% off but max £5, a £50 order gets £5 off not £10
Applies To → Merchant
Search and select which merchant this applies to — must be selected before the Apply To product/category field activates
Apply To
Once merchant is selected: choose specific products or categories to discount
Minimum Purchase Requirements
No minimum requirements
Discount applies regardless of order value
Minimum purchase amount
Customer must spend at least £X before the discount applies
Minimum quantity
Customer must add at least N items before the discount applies
Discount Type 2

Order Discount

Reduces the total amount of the entire order — not tied to specific products. Best for general promotional codes (e.g. 10% off your next order).

Fig OD.1 — Order Discount: same Discount Type / Code / Settings section as all other types

Fig OD.2 — Discount Value: Type (Percentage), Percentage % field, Max Discount Amount. Applies To: Apply to all merchants toggle (ON) showing "This discount will apply to all merchants of your store." Minimum Purchase Requirements

Fig OD.3 — Eligible Customers and Eligible Order Types & Payment Methods sections

Fig OD.4 — Usage Limits, Combine with other discounts (Product / Order / Delivery fee discounts), Active Dates

Order Discount — Unique Fields
Discount Value → Type
Percentage or Fixed Amount
Max Discount Amount
Optional cap on the discount — e.g. 20% off but maximum £10
Applies To → Apply to all merchants
Toggle ON (default) = applies across all merchants on the platform. Toggle OFF to restrict to specific merchants
ℹ️
Order Discounts reduce the subtotal of the order. They do not affect delivery fees — use a Delivery Fee Discount for that, and enable stacking if you want both to apply together.
Discount Type 3

Delivery Fee Discount

Offers free or reduced delivery fees. Can be applied across all merchants or specific ones. Great for first-order incentives or loyalty rewards.

Fig DD.1 — Delivery Fee Discount: Discount Type / Code / Settings (same as all types)

Fig DD.2 — Discount Value: Free Delivery radio option (Customers get free delivery on their orders). Applies To: Apply to all merchants toggle ON

Fig DD.3 — Minimum Purchase Requirements: No minimum / Minimum purchase amount / Minimum quantity. Eligible Customers section

Fig DD.4 — Eligible Order Types & Payment Methods, Set discount usage limits, Combine with other discounts (Product discounts / Order discounts), Active Dates

Delivery Fee Discount — Unique Fields
Discount Value → Free Delivery
Select this radio to give completely free delivery. Alternative is a percentage or fixed reduction on the delivery fee
Applies To → Apply to all merchants
Toggle ON = free/discounted delivery applies across all merchants. Toggle OFF to restrict to specific merchants
Combine
Can be stacked with Product discounts and/or Order discounts if those options are ticked
💡
For a "Free delivery on your first order" campaign: set Discount Type to Coupon Code, set Eligible Customers to Specific Segments (create a "New Customers" segment), set Limit uses per customer to 1, and enable Free Delivery.
Discount Type 4

Buy X Get Y

Reward customers who buy specific quantities or spend a minimum amount — they receive a free or discounted item in return. A powerful upsell and loyalty tool.

Fig BX.1 — Buy X Get Y Configuration: Merchant Selection (search & select merchant first — required for both Customer Buys and Customer Gets sections). Customer Buys: Minimum quantity of items or Minimum purchase amount (£50 shown). Any items from dropdown. Select Products dropdown

Fig BX.2 — Customer Gets: Quantity field (default 1). Any items from dropdown (greyed until merchant selected). Select Products. At a Discounted Value: Percentage / Amount off each / Free (selected). Discount Summary shows: Buy X: Spend £50 from 0 products, get 1 item(s) for FREE

Buy X Get Y — Unique Fields
Merchant Selection Required
Must select a merchant first — both Customer Buys and Customer Gets sections are locked until a merchant is chosen. Both sections use products from this merchant
Customer Buys → Minimum quantity
Customer must add at least N items from the specified products/categories
Customer Buys → Minimum purchase amount
Customer must spend at least £X from the specified products/categories
Customer Buys → Any items from
Restrict qualifying products to a specific category
Customer Buys → Select Products
Further narrow qualifying items to specific products
Customer Gets → Quantity
How many free/discounted items the customer receives — default 1
Customer Gets → Any items from
Which category the reward item comes from
Customer Gets → Select Products
Specific product(s) the customer receives
At a Discounted Value → Percentage
Customer gets the reward item at X% off
At a Discounted Value → Amount off each
Customer gets the reward item at £X off per item
At a Discounted Value → Free
Customer gets the reward item completely free — most common setup
ℹ️
The Discount Summary panel updates live — verify it reads exactly as intended before saving. Example: "Spend £50 from 0 products, get 1 item(s) for FREE" — make sure the product count is correct (not 0) before going live.
💡
Buy X Get Y is merchant-specific. If you need the same deal across multiple merchants, you must create a separate coupon for each merchant.
Yolulo

Hyperzod Platform · Promotions & Coupons SOP · v1.0 · March 2026

Internal Use Only — Popups, Push Notifications & Highlight covered in a separate SOP
